expectations.text: Reads DeploymentMode (or equivalent) from config rather than hardcoding the mode — e.g. IsSaaS() helper or ConfigurationHelper.GetConfigSetting("DeploymentMode")
expectations.evidence: IsSaaS() reads ConfigurationHelper.GetConfigSetting("DeploymentMode") and compares string.Equals(mode, "SaaS", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase). Config key set in CXCustomizations.config.

expectations.text: Job row is always inserted into the job table (cx_job or similar) regardless of SaaS vs OnPrem — both paths insert first for tracking/auditing
expectations.evidence: Step 5 Submit() shows: var jobDbKey = _commandFactory.InsertJob(...) called unconditionally before the if(isSaaS) branch. Comment explicitly says 'Always insert the job — both modes'.

expectations.text: On the SaaS branch, the process proc is called immediately in the same web request (synchronous path) — not deferred to a service
expectations.evidence: if (isSaaS) { _commandFactory.ProcessJob(jobDbKey); } immediately after InsertJob. ProcessJob calls cx_pog_copy_process stored proc in-process.

expectations.text: On the OnPrem branch, only the insert happens — processing is deferred to a background service/Automator that polls the job table
expectations.evidence: Comment: '// OnPrem: return immediately; Automator/service polls cx_job'. No ProcessJob call on the OnPrem path. Process proc's NULL path designed for the background service.
